title: Software Engineer - Frontend – Helsing
content:
## Who we are

Helsing is a defence AI company. Our mission is to protect our democracies. We aim to achieve technological leadership, so that open societies can continue to make sovereign decisions and control their ethical standards.

As democracies, we believe we have a special responsibility to be thoughtful about the development and deployment of powerful technologies like AI. We take this responsibility seriously.

We are an ambitious and committed team of engineers, AI specialists and customer-facing programme managers. We are looking for mission-driven people to join our European teams – and apply their skills to solve the most complex and impactful problems. We embrace an open and transparent culture that welcomes healthy debates on the use of technology in defence, its benefits, and its ethical implications.

## The role

Collaborating closely with our dynamic team of product designers and engineers, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the foundation of frontend at Helsing. Embracing a hands-on approach, you'll not only lead by example, but also spearhead the development and expansion of a tight-knit team that works across a wide-range of internal teams and products. Your primary emphasis will be on enhancing usability, creating re-usable foundations for multiple front-ends and optimising the end-user experience across the entire company.

## The day-to-day

* Review code contributions, proposals, and RFCs from colleagues. At Helsing we strongly believe that unblocking others takes precedence over spending more time on your own work.
* Own the user interface end-to-end, from early wireframes and prototypes through to performant, production-ready code. You'll work closely with product and UX colleagues to shape how users interact with our tools. We have relatively few frontend engineers at Helsing; this means that we operate at a high level of autonomy and there are plenty of opportunities for impactful work.
* Propose and implement new features, capabilities, tooling, visualizations, or even full products across our stack. We encourage our engineers to look beyond their immediate team for missed opportunities, and to speak up when they identify one.
* Establish and evolve our React component library, ensuring consistency, reusability, and alignment with our visual design standards across multiple front-ends.
* Collaborate with others beyond your team and share knowledge with the wider frontend community at Helsing. Contribute to establishing our frontend engineering standards. Build or adopt tools that help enforce these standards, or make our frontend engineers more efficient in a different way.
* Become a mentor, find a mentee, and help support and develop our interviewing pipeline. The people make or break the company, and we want as many engineers as possible to be part of fostering and growing the organisation.
* Share your experience and technical know-how with other Helsingers. We host tech talks, get together for smaller "le

title: Frontify - Engineering Team Lead - Brand Fundamentals
content:
## Engineering Team Lead - Brand Fundamentals

Sankt Gallen Metropolitan Area

Product – Engineering /

Full-time /

Remote

apply for this job

We're all about helping brands turn ideas into impact.

Frontify’s brand platform transforms how teams organize digital assets, collaborate on projects, and create engaging campaigns. Our people empower thousands of marketers and designers — including teams at Uber, Microsoft, Volkswagen, and Telefónica — to build engaging brands.

With headquarters in St. Gallen, Switzerland, and offices in London and New York City, we share a vibrant culture built on creativity, collaboration, inclusion, and joy. And we’re on the lookout for new team members to share our vision. If you’re ready for a brand-new adventure, keep reading!

**Your team**

Your new team is a group of motivated, collaborative, and innovative Engineers. Working on-site, hybrid, or fully remote, they come together to create and evolve a well-loved product but always keep room for new ideas and approaches. Outside of work, they’re parents, gamers, bookworms, adrenaline junkies, and so much more.

**Your mission**

Our team, Brand Fundamentals, owns the brand domain, and our mission is to ensure that the brand truth can be reliably consumed by humans, systems, and AI. We constantly raise the bar and build the tools that help our customers maintain consistency, while growing their brands.

You’ll lead the Brand Fundamentals teams and play a critical role in elevating our team's performance and capabilities. When leading both backend and frontend engineers in your team, you’ll need a deep understanding of how to architect, plan, and drive implementation in a SaaS product at scale. You will drive technical excellence and support the team in delivering impactful solutions.

### Your responsibilities

* Lead a software development team, mentor engineers, inspire high performance, and foster team cohesion.
* Mentor the engineers in your team and guide them to grow.
* Collaborate effectively with product managers, designers, and cross-functional stakeholders to drive outcomes and handle uncertainty.
* Contribute to product decisions, plan delivery cycles, and drive the team toward measurable, successful outcomes.
* Actively participate in hands-on development, working alongside engineers to design, implement, and review changes; take ownership of delivering high-quality software on time, proactively managing risks and dependencies.
* Architect scalable solutions, guide technical vision, and balance innovation with technical debt.

### Your story

* **You can work remotely from anywhere in Switzerland or on-site in our St. Gallen office – or even mix it up during the week!** Choose where to work from that works best for you.
* You’ve led software development teams for at least 2 years, mentoring engineers, inspiring high performance and fostering a cohesive team.
* You have strong experience in web-application backend development, in languages such as PH

## Senior Product Engineer - Integrations

apply for this job

We're all about helping brands turn ideas into impact.

Frontify’s brand platform transforms how teams organize digital assets, collaborate on projects, and create engaging campaigns. Our people empower thousands of marketers and designers — including teams at Uber, Microsoft, Volkswagen, and Telefónica — to build engaging brands.

With headquarters in St. Gallen, Switzerland, and offices in London and New York City, we share a vibrant culture built on creativity, collaboration, inclusion, and joy. And we’re on the lookout for new team members to share our vision. If you’re ready for a brand-new adventure, keep reading!

**Your team**

Your new team is the **Brand Ecosystem / Integrations team**, reporting into Product. We operate at the intersection of Product, Engineering, Design, and Partnerships.

We’re responsible for how Frontify connects to the outside world — through apps, integrations, APIs, and experimental ecosystem features. The team is currently PM-led, and you will be the first senior technical builder shaping the foundation before we grow further.

Outside of work, we’re parents, gamers, cyclists, hikers, LEGO collectors, and more — but at work we care deeply about craft, clarity, and ownership.

### Your mission

You’ll help shape how Frontify connects to the wider world, turning our platform into a seamless part of our customers’ daily workflows. Your work won’t live in isolation; it will extend across tools, teams, and ecosystems where brands actually operate.

As a Senior Product Engineer – Integrations, you’ll take ownership of building and evolving integrations, apps, and platform capabilities that bring Frontify closer to the tools our customers rely on. You’ll transform ambiguous opportunities into scalable, high-quality solutions by combining strong engineering fundamentals with product thinking and a sharp UX instinct.

You’ll also help define how we explore and validate new ecosystem ideas, creating the foundations that allow partners to build confidently on top of Frontify. This is a role for someone who wants to build beyond features and shape systems, standards, and experiences that scale.

* You’ll own and evolve business-critical first-party integrations (e.g., Figma, desktop applications), ensuring they remain reliable, scalable, and easy to use.

* You’ll turn ambiguous product opportunities into structured solutions, from early exploration to production-ready delivery.

title: Applied AI Engineer | Revolut United Kingdom
content:
# Applied AI Engineer

Office: Barcelona · Dubai · Lisbon · London · MadridRemote: Cyprus  · Czech Republic  · Ireland  · Porto  · Portugal  · Serbia  · Singapore · South Africa

### About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth, 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

### About the role

The Product team shapes the experiences that power Revolut. They turn complex problems into simple, scalable solutions — collaborating across Design, Engineering, and Data to build features our customers love.

We’re looking for a Lead Applied AI Engineer who can manage their product roadmap, including day-to-day operations, project execution, and risk assessment.

From conception to launch and beyond, you’ll collaborate cross-functionally and communicate with senior management to be the go-to person for your product.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

### What you'll be doing

* Owning the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for AI-powered solutions, leveraging LLMs and other machine learning technologies
* Collaborating closely with Data Scientists and Software Engineers to deliver impactful solutions
* Leading cross-functional teams to build and deploy NLP and AI solutions, such as AI assistants and AI automation platforms
* Setting your team's goals, success metrics, and roadmap to align with company goals
* Delivering measurable results for sophisticated products under ambiguous situations in a fast-paced environment

### What you'll need

* Experience developing and launching AI products/features from scratch
* High analytical and technical acumen, along with solid business sense and problem-solving skills
* Experience in a data science or machine learning role
* Experience owning machine learning projects, from design to implementation and iteration
* Great Python, SQL, or other programming language skills
* Hands-on experience with deep learning libs (TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, etc.)
* Knowledge of version control, model deployment, and developer environments
* Evidence of impressive mathematical and statistical knowledge
* An entrepreneurial drive to never settle

*Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why
stack: React.js, Next.js, Python, Node.js, LLM Integration

title: Senior Frontend Engineer (m/f/d) | Jobs at Jedox GmbH
content:
# Senior Frontend Engineer (m/f/d)

Germany remote

Full-time

Permanent employee

## What is Jedox?

At Jedox, we offer cutting-edge Performance Management Platform and Solutions that enable businesses to plan, analyze, and optimize their financial and operational performance. Our B2B Platform-as-a-Service offering consists of various components - sophisticated data representation and data collection, a powerful planning engine, and data integration - developed by our specialized product teams.  
  
To drive our “Digital Business Partner” vision, we’re expanding our Engineering team and looking for a Full-Stack Software Engineer to help design and build the next generation of our platform services and applications.

## Your Responsibilities

As a Senior Frontend Engineer, you’ll design, build, and maintain scalable, cloud-ready web applications using React, TypeScript, and with micro-frontend architecture. You’ll take ownership across the modern frontends and system integrations - while driving code quality, CI/CD automation, and maintainable architecture. You’ll collaborate with Product Manager, UX/UI designer, QA in a cross-functional team to clarify requirements, align on priorities, and deliver value through efficient agile development. You’ll also mentor engineers through code reviews and knowledge sharing and contribute to cross-team technical initiatives that shape scalable, resilient systems.   
  
**What You’ll Do**  

* **Design, develop, and maintain platform UI components and services** using React, TypeScript and other modern frontend technologies.
* **Implement CI/CD pipelines and ensure code quality** by writing tests, enforcing standards, and maintaining deployment automation with tools like GitHub, Jenkins/GitHub Actions, and SonarQube.
* **Collaborate with stakeholders** to align on technical and strategic priorities, clarify requirements, and plan cross-functional work. Communication via tickets system, Microsoft Teams, and regular agile ceremonies.
* **Mentor junior frontend engineers** - providing technical guidance, conducting code reviews, and supporting professional development to improve overall engineering output.
* **Contribute to system and application architecture** - making design decisions for scalable and maintainable systems; evaluate and introduce new technologies where appropriate; **push cross-team technical initiatives** and coordinate efforts across teams.

## Your Profile

* 8+ years of professional experience as a Frontend or Full-Stack Engineer.
* Proven experience with a focus on quality, clean code, and team collaboration.
* Demonstrated track record partnering with product managers, UX/UI designers, QA, and business stakeholders on technical initiatives.
* Proficiency in modern technologies, e.g., React, TypeScript/JavaScript, etc. Strong frontend skills: HTML, CSS, responsive design, and modern UI frameworks.
* Extensive experience with scalable (micro-)frontend architectures and cloud-ready applicati

## Company Description

We are seeking a technically skilled and passionate Senior-level Data Scientist with expertise in machine learning methods to join our international and dynamic Data Science department at AUTO1 Group Technology. AUTO1 Group is Europe's leading digital automotive platform. We revolutionise the used car market with our well-known brands Autohero, wirkaufendeinauto.de, and AUTO1.com. * Possibility to work remotely, either partially or on a full-time basis

Your mission and role will be building and maintaining a platform that enables everyone at Wise to rapidly experiment and productionise Generative AI based systems, providing tooling, guidance and guardrails so that teams can ensure the safety of our customers and their data.

**How we work**

We operate with a belief in automation, programmatic implementation, and reusable design. We’re looking for people who can step back and think holistically about the ecosystem, but also follow through and help implement the design; drawing on the resources across the rest of the Platform Tribe.

As we grow, it is imperative to protect our customers’ money and navigate complex regulatory requirements world-wide. You will have a focus on running and maintaining services, making sure we have the right observability and service management elements in place.

We need to sustain this growth by continuously iterating on the services we run, with a focus on availability, security, and ease of use. Hallo! Welcome to our Berlin office, located in the European capital for innovation and digitization.

## About us

Welcome to Pipedrive Germany, our vibrant new hub right in the heart of Berlin!

Opened on November 23, 2021, it’s now home to 30+ professionals from 15+ countries, all united by a passion for marketing and product development.

## Our traditions

At Pipedrive Berlin, we believe that a strong team is built not just on passion for our work, but also on the joy of spending time together. With our diverse roots and cultural backgrounds, we bond over cuisine evenings, sharing everything from tasty tacos to Georgian dumplings. Plus, we love to bring back tasty souvenirs from our travels – nothing brings a team together like food!

We’re all about exploring the city and soaking in the local culture, too! Join us for PipeHikes in the Berlin countryside, scenic boat trips along the Spree and annual Oktoberfest celebrations at our favorite brewery. We’ll also end the year on a high with our winter award show featuring laughter, creative sketches and special appearances from our kids, pets, and even our customers!

Our office is nestled in the bustling Mitte district, boasting incredible public transport links and an endless array of amazing restaurants and coffee spots for food lovers. Our bright and lively office space is designed to spark creativity and camaraderie, complete with comfy couches, an Xbox, and a ping-pong table in our All Hands area – perfect for game nights and team bonding. And yes, we’re a dog-friendly office, so your furry friend can join the fun!

Step into our cozy meeting rooms, each uniquely named after our favorite local dishes (including Currywurst and Döner), where teammates come together to brainstorm and collaborate. content:
# Tallinn

Welcome to Tallinn, where Pipedrive was born.

Come and join Pipedrive’s Tallinn team!

See open positions

## About us

Pipedrive was born in Tallinn. What started as a small startup in a shared office has grown into an international company with an entire building in Tallinn’s vibrant WoHo quarter. Today, Tallinn is home to our engineering and product hub, where 300+ team members from 30+ nationalities work together – many of them engineers.

Pipedrive is proud to be Estonia’s fifth unicorn and has consistently been recognized as one of the best places in the country to work since 2016, according to both the media and our employees. And we’re not just about jobs – at Pipedrive, you’ll have the chance to grow, learn and make a real impact by helping shape a product used by more than 100,000 companies worldwide.

## Our traditions

At Pipedrive, we love to celebrate the moments that matter – from birthdays and work anniversaries to promotions. One of our most beloved traditions is getting together for *Kringle*, a classic Estonian pastry, though you’ll also see pizza, donuts or pancakes make an appearance. To make sure no celebration goes unnoticed, we’ve got a kitchen-alert Slack channel to keep everyone in the loop.

Our biggest annual event is the Summer Garage – a full-day celebration that brings teams together to connect, unwind and enjoy live music performed by our very own band (and sometimes a guest artist or two).

We also host occasional hackathons and innovation accelerators to spark new ideas for improving the product and how we work together.

We believe in a balance of fun and focus. From Lightning Talks where anyone can practice their presentation skills with a topic they care about, to Sudoku tournaments and gaming challenges organized by teammates, there’s always something to get involved in.

We’re also proud to contribute to the wider community: Our team actively supports the local engineering, product and HR scenes in Estonia by hosting in-house meetups, participating in conferences and speaking at events.

Staying active outside of work is also a big part of our culture. Whether it's a friendly game of table tennis, a disc golf match or a padel session – one of the most popular sports among our Tallinn team – there's always fun going on.

## Where you’ll find us

You’ll find us in Tallinn’s modern WoHo district, where our custom-designed office makes it easy to feel at home. Each of our eight themed floors – park, nature, sports, IT, music, art, movies and culture – is fully immersive. Whether you’re jamming on the music floor’s instruments or exploring the art gallery, there’s something for everyone.

The heart of the office is our all-hands area, where people gather for a coffee or something else from our well stocked kitchens. Since 2010, Pipedrive has been on a mission to support sales and marketing teams with easy-to-use, powerful tools that make everyday work faster and easier. Today, our cloud-based software is trusted by over 100,000 companies and used in 179 countries. We have grown from a five-person team to a truly international company of over 850+ people, representing more than 50 nationalities, with ten offices distributed across Europe and the US. In 2020, Pipedrive received a majority investment from Vista Equity Partners, a global investment firm that invests exclusively in enterprise software, data and technology-enabled businesses, making Pipedrive the fifth unicorn from Estonia.
